uests":{"storage":"10Gi"}}}}]}}

  generation: 1
  name: mongodb-replica
  namespace: mongodb
  resourceVersion: "923017"
  uid: 4b4e1b2b-e24d-421d-a7e6-2d588ce76c2e
spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-replica
  serviceName: mongodb-replica-svc
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: mongodb-replica
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - mongod
        - --replSet
        - mongodb-replica
        - --bind_ip_all
        - --auth
        - --keyFile=/etc/mongo-keyfile/keyfile
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        image: mongo:6.0.5
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        livenessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /usr/local/bin/health-check.sh
          failureThreshold: 10
          initialDelaySeconds: 90
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 5
        name: mongod
        ports:
        - containerPort: 27017
          name: mongodb
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /usr/local/bin/health-check.sh
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 5
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 512Mi

        - mountPath: /etc/mongo-keyfile
          name: mongo-keyfile
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /usr/local/bin/health-check.sh
          name: health-script
          readOnly: true
          subPath: health-check.sh
        - mountPath: /etc/health
          name: health-secret
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /etc/health/overrides
          name: health-overrides
          readOnly: true
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext:
        runAsGroup: 0
        runAsUser: 0
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - name: mongo-keyfile
        secret:
          defaultMode: 256
          secretName: mongo-keyfile
      - name: health-secret
        secret:
          defaultMode: 420
          items:
          - key: password
            path: health-password
          secretName: health-user-password
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 420
          name: health-overrides
        name: health-overrides
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 493

      partition: 0

  volumeClaimTemplates:

    metadata:

content.thinking: The readiness probe is failing with: "MongoServerError: Authentication failed." The probe uses `/usr/local/bin/health-check.sh`. Let me look at the health check script, the health-user-password secret, and the health-overrides configmap to understand what's happening.

content.text: The readiness probe fails with "Authentication failed." Let me inspect the health check script and related resources.

input.command: kubectl get configmap health-check-script -n mongodb -o yaml 2>&1

content.content:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  health-check.sh: |
    #!/bin/sh
    set -e
    PW_FILE="/etc/health/health-password"
    OVERRIDE="/etc/health/overrides/${POD_NAME}"
    if [ -f "$OVERRIDE" ]; then
      HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$OVERRIDE")"
    else
      HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$PW_FILE")"
    fi
    mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:${HEALTH_PW}@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' >/dev/null
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"health-check.sh":"#!/bin/sh\nset -e\nPW_FILE=\"/etc/health/health-password\"\nOVERRIDE=\"/etc/health/overrides/${POD_NAME}\"\nif [ -f \"$OVERRIDE\" ]; then\n  HEALTH_PW=\"$(cat \"$OVERRIDE\")\"\nelse\n  HEALTH_PW=\"$(cat \"$PW_FILE\")\"\nfi\nmongosh --quiet \"mongodb://health-user:${HEALTH_PW}@localhost:27017/admin\" --eval 'db.hello().ok' \u003e/dev/null\n"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"health-check-script","namespace":"mongodb"}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T15:36:05Z"
  name: health-check-script
  namespace: mongodb
  resourceVersion: "922795"
  uid: f52d97f0-03a2-48d9-a1e8-1641b2c2d525

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. The health check script for `mongodb-replica-1` uses the override password `legacyHealthPass123` (from the `health-overrides` ConfigMap), but the actual health-user in MongoDB for that pod might be using `healthpass123` (from the secret).

Let me understand the flow:
1. The `health-check.sh` script checks if an override file exists at `/etc/health/overrides/${POD_NAME}`
2. For `mongodb-replica-1`, it finds the override file with password `legacyHealthPass123`
3. But the actual health-user password in MongoDB is `healthpass123`

So the probe is trying to authenticate with `legacyHealthPass123` when the real password is `healthpass123`. The solution is to remove that stale override from the ConfigMap. Let me verify this by checking the actual MongoDB state and the replica set status from one of the healthy pods.

content.text: Found the issue: `mongodb-replica-1` has a stale override password (`legacyHealthPass123`) in the `health-overrides` ConfigMap, but the actual health-user password is `healthpass123`. Let me verify the MongoDB state first.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello()' 2>&1 | head -20
input.description: Check replica set state from pod-0 without auth

content.thinking:
`mongodb-replica-1` is the current PRIMARY. So the replica set IS operational but `mongodb-replica-1` is not Ready because the health check is using the wrong password. The override password `legacyHealthPass123` doesn't match the actual health-user password `healthpass123`.

Let me verify this by trying to connect to pod-1 with both passwords.

content.text: Pod-1 is the PRIMARY. The readiness probe fails because the `health-overrides` ConfigMap has a stale password `legacyHealthPass123` for `mongodb-replica-1`, but the actual health-user password is `healthpass123`. Let me confirm:

content.thinking: Confirmed! The `legacyHealthPass123` password fails, and `healthpass123` succeeds. The fix is to remove the stale override for `mongodb-replica-1` from the `health-overrides` ConfigMap. This will make the health check script fall back to using the secret's password (`healthpass123`), which is correct.

content.text: Confirmed. The fix is to remove the stale `mongodb-replica-1` entry from the `health-overrides` ConfigMap so the probe falls back to the correct password from the secret.

input.description: Remove stale password override for mongodb-replica-1

content.text: Now wait for the readiness probe to pass (it runs every 10s with a 60s initial delay already passed):

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 12); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get pod mongodb-replica-1 -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Ready")].status}' 2>&1)
  echo "$(date +%T) mongodb-replica-1 Ready: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "True" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 10
done

input.command:
# Discover the current primary dynamically
PRIMARY_POD=$(kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().primary' 2>&1 | tr -d '"' | cut -d'.' -f1)
echo "Current primary: $PRIMARY_POD"
kubectl exec -n mongodb "$PRIMARY_POD" -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'rs.status().members.map(m => ({name: m.name, state: m.stateStr}))' 2>&1
input.description: Discover primary and check replica set member states

input.command:
ADMIN_PW=$(kubectl get secret admin-user-password -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
echo "Admin password: $ADMIN_PW"
kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-1 -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://admin-user:${ADMIN_PW}@localhost:27017/admin" \
  --eval 'rs.status().members.map(m => ({name: m.name, state: m.stateStr}))' 2>&1
input.description: Check replica set member states with admin auth
